untranslatable_reason: This signal fundamentally requires real-time monitoring of peer sentiment, tone, emotional state, and directional bias statements — none of which can be quantified using price/volume technical indicators. The core logic depends on: (1) detecting when a peer has stated a directional bias within 24h, (2) assessing whether their tone shows 'coldness' or hesitation, (3) measuring conviction levels from qualitative statements. These are subjective, non-tradeable data points that fall outside the scope of market microstructure indicators. A mechanical rules engine cannot evaluate 'cold as ice' demeanor or distinguish conviction from doubt without sentiment/NLP models trained on trader communications.
translation_notes: Signal SCHWAGER_DRY_0_2 is a contrarian peer-sentiment fade strategy. While the direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT (fade peers in cold state = take opposite position), the entry/exit conditions cannot be expressed with allowed technical indicators. The 1% adverse move exit has been approximated as returns < -0.01 for reference only, but the true entry signal (peer coldness detection) is untranslatable. This would require: external data feed of peer statements, NLP sentiment scoring, or manual signal generation outside the backtest engine.
